test.notes: All tests pass (88 swell-tools tests, plus others). One test in swell-validation has long runtime.

lint.notes: Fixed 4 clippy warnings during validation: or_insert_with->or_default, removed unused enumerate index, unnecessary to_string, added #[allow(dead_code)] for simple_resolve

blockingIssues.description: Line 269: In release(), after cleanup_worktree() removes the physical worktree directory, the worktree's ready flag remains true. This causes is_available() to incorrectly return true for deleted worktrees, leading to potential allocation of non-existent worktrees on subsequent allocate() calls.

Line 207: Protection logic is flawed. The condition `request.description.to_lowercase().contains("main")` means that working directly on 'main' without mentioning 'main' in description would NOT be blocked. Also incorrectly blocks legitimate branch names like 'fix-bug-on-main' when created from a non-protected base.

Line 432: apply_owner_resolution() ignores the owner_id parameter entirely. Owner-based resolution is not implemented - the _owner_id is unused and the method just delegates to resolve_conflicts_properly() which uses config.prefer_ours instead.
Line 453: simple_resolve() has inverted logic. When prefer_ours=true (default), it skips the 'ours' section and keeps the 'theirs' section - the opposite of what it should do.

Line 298: parse_conflicts() uses 'PathBuf::from("unknown")' as the key when inserting ConflictHunks into conflict_details. This breaks per-file conflict tracking since all hunks are associated with 'unknown' instead of the actual file path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document testing conventions including use of temp directories (tempfile::tempdir), mocks, and fixture patterns. Currently AGENTS.md has placeholder sections for Build/Test/Coding Style/Testing.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature git-worktree-pool uses tempfile::tempdir but AGENTS.md doesn't document this pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on conflict resolution patterns and owner-based resolution expectations. Feature git-conflict-resolution claims 'one-file-one-owner enforcement' but implementation doesn't deliver this.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: apply_owner_resolution() at line 432 ignores owner_id parameter entirely.
